title: Draba thomasii
taxonomy: Draba thomasii is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family includes a wide variety of plants, such as broccoli, cauliflower, and mustard. Draba thomasii is further classified within the genus Draba, which comprises small, annual or perennial plants. The genus Draba is native to the Northern Hemisphere, with species found in North America, Europe, and Asia. Draba thomasii is specifically a species of Draba native to the western United States.
uses: Draba thomasii, also known as Thomas' whitlowgrass, is a plant species with potential medicinal uses. Although there is limited information available, it is said to have anti-inflammatory properties.
